comment.body:
Alma, your character really shows through your caring behaviour, always taking ample time to explain yourself time and time again to everyone. Even today i think I saw you consoling someone here for taking losses. imo you shouldnt even feel the need to defend yourself against the critics, anyone who trades for a living knows that the markets are always waiting for the next opportunity to fill you with some humility. NO ONE can predict everything. Reflexivity rules the financial markets. 

You said yourself many times you are just sharing your view, observations. That is what most of us are here for. 

I continue to find nuggets daily in your macro economic and micro vol structure posts. I am very grateful that I was lucky enough to join this sub during this exciting times in financial markets, as we all experience the shifts of a new world order.

comment.body: So, if I’m understanding this correctly, the bond market was about to blow up so Bessent and co let out some steam with the tariff announcement to avoid a blow-up, get vix down, and set off a short-squeeze. Yet at the same time, we now have 125% tariffs on China, 25% on Mexico/Canada, and possibly others? Maybe EU as well since they passed retaliatory tariffs, and 10% on everyone else? Seems like a good opportunity to get the fuck out of the market if one was caught in this drawdown. Would love to hear other thoughts/opinions and why I might be wrong in my assessment.
